office_does: A State Representative writes and votes on Washington state law and the operating, capital, and transportation budgets, covering school funding, tax structure, housing mandates, health funding, and criminal-justice statutes.
race_blurb: Nine-year incumbent Democrat Shelley Kloba faces an intra-party challenge from Bothell Deputy Mayor Jenne Alderks, who cites the 1st LD Democrats' sole endorsement, while Libertarian Jeff Lyon and Republican Cliff Moon contest the right-of-center lane with low-resource campaigns. The top two vote-getters advance to the general election regardless of party.

candidates.summary: Incumbent State Representative since 2017, Vice-Chair of the House Technology, Economic Development, and Veterans Committee, with a legislative focus on data privacy, AI consumer protection, and gambling and cannabis regulation.
highlights: Incumbent since 2017; Vice-Chair of the House Technology, Economic Development, and Veterans Committee and founder of the House Technology caucus [S1]
Sponsor of the People's Privacy Act and passed 2019 legislation creating a statewide gambling self-exclusion program [S3]
Says she will fix the regressive tax code and close tax loopholes, redirecting the revenue to schools [S2]
Endorsed by the WA State Labor Council, Sierra Club, WEA PAC, and Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates [S1], plus House Speaker Laurie Jinkins per her campaign site [S2]
Leads the field in reported fundraising with $38.5k across 88 contributions [S4]

taxes.basis: Repeatedly pledges to fix the regressive tax code and close loopholes to redirect revenue to schools, without proposing broader new wealth taxes.

spending.basis: Platform emphasizes fully funding K-12, renewable-energy investment, and expanded behavioral-health access rather than audits or restraint.

safety.basis: Frames public safety around increasing access to behavioral health care rather than enforcement measures.

climate.basis: Supports green-jobs creation, renewable-energy investment, and waterway monitoring, and carries a Sierra Club endorsement.

experience.basis: Nine-plus years in the House with passed legislation, committee leadership, and endorsements including the House Speaker — the maximal proven-record profile in this field.

reform.basis: Campaigns on institutional coalition support — labor, party organizations, and elected officials including the House Speaker — rather than structural political reform.

healthcare.basis: Emphasizes increasing access to behavioral health care within existing structures; no universal-coverage proposal.

social.basis: Pledges to protect civil rights and is endorsed by Planned Parenthood Alliance Advocates and the Alliance for Gun Responsibility.

tech.basis: Legislative record centers on data privacy regulation: sponsor of the People's Privacy Act, reining in data brokers, and reducing AI harms to consumers.

candidates.summary: Bothell Deputy Mayor and human services specialist in behavioral health, mounting an intra-party challenge to the Democratic incumbent on a platform of affordability, universal healthcare, and her municipal record.
highlights: Bothell Deputy Mayor, first elected to the City Council in 2021 and re-elected in 2025; former Planning Commission member [S1]
Cites her Bothell record on crisis-response programs and community court public-safety approaches [S2]
States she received the 1st LD Democrats' sole endorsement over the incumbent [S2]

spending.basis: Platform centers on expanding housing opportunities, transit investment, and universal healthcare rather than audits or restraint.

housing.basis: Supports expanding housing opportunities and serves on the board of a homelessness-services nonprofit; specific mechanism (supply vs. tenant protection) not stated.

safety.basis: Cites crisis-response programs and community court alternatives from her Bothell record as her public-safety approach.

experience.basis: Challenger to a nine-year incumbent offering municipal-executive and human-services experience rather than a legislative record.

healthcare.basis: Explicitly supports guaranteeing universal healthcare coverage in both her pamphlet statement and campaign site.

social.basis: Emphasizes protecting the rights, freedoms, and human dignity of all Washingtonians; former LGBTQIA+ youth group leader.

candidates.summary: Libertarian candidate from Woodinville, a disabled Navy veteran, former deputy sheriff, and cybersecurity engineering manager running on tax elimination for households under $125,000 and direct-democracy mechanisms.
highlights: Proposes eliminating state taxes for most families earning under $125,000 and requiring direct voter approval for any income tax [S1]
Opposes top-down state zoning mandates and supports reforming the Growth Management Act to increase housing supply [S2]
Disabled Navy veteran, former Chesapeake, Virginia deputy sheriff, and cybersecurity engineering manager [S1]
Supports binding citizen initiatives and decriminalizing victimless conduct while holding offenders accountable [S2]

taxes.basis: Platform centers on eliminating state taxes for most sub-$125k households and requiring voter approval for any income tax.

housing.basis: Would eliminate regulations he says drive up housing costs and reform the GMA to increase market supply.

safety.basis: Genuinely mixed: pairs decriminalizing victimless conduct with holding offenders accountable, citing his deputy-sheriff experience.

climate.basis: Mixed posture: supports advanced nuclear and expanded 'cleanest, most efficient' power while also proposing closed-loop cooling requirements for data centers; no stated position on carbon pricing.

experience.basis: No prior elected office and describes himself as proudly unaffiliated with the political establishment — the maximal outsider case.

local-control.basis: Explicitly opposes top-down state zoning mandates and argues communities should not be taxed for projects that do not benefit them.

reform.basis: Supports binding citizen initiatives immune to legal or bureaucratic reversal and runs an anti-establishment, near-zero-fundraising campaign.

tech.basis: Would require closed-loop cooling for data centers to reduce freshwater use — a targeted regulation, though he is otherwise a tech-industry executive promoting AI career pathways.

candidates.summary: Republican candidate with decades in the construction trades and small-business ownership, running on property-tax relief, full funding for local police, and reduced business regulation.
highlights: Centers property-tax relief, saying layered taxes drive up the cost of gas, groceries, and utilities [S1]
Supports law enforcement, full funding for local police, and criminal-justice accountability [S1]
Reports 63 years in the construction trades and 54 years of small-business ownership; endorsed by the King County Republican Party [S1]

taxes.basis: Platform centers on property-tax relief and argues layered taxes drive up the cost of gas, groceries, and utilities.

safety.basis: Runs on supporting law enforcement, fully funding local police, and criminal-justice accountability.

experience.basis: No elected experience beyond Precinct Committee Officer; offers decades of construction-trades and small-business background instead of government service.

immigration.basis: States support for legal immigration, an indirect signal of an enforcement-oriented posture; no detailed position given.

social.basis: Says schools should focus on academics and parents should raise their children — a parental-rights framing.
